name: poly_bevel_S1_group_segs2
description: Polygon Bevel (mesh.bevel, edit-mode Polygons) GROUP x SEGMENTS interaction -- task 0458 Phase 1 (+S1), mesh.d's own pre-existing doc comment marked this combination 'KNOWN-UNTESTED'. Two adjacent cube faces (+Y top, +Z front, the same pair as poly_bevel_corner.json's sibling poly_bevel_two_faces_grouped case) grouped (group=true) AND segmented (segs=2), inset=0.25, shift=0.15. Reference topology: 8v/6f -> 20v/18f -- the group-shared ridge corner is SEGMENTED THE SAME WAY as every other boundary vertex (equal linear lerp from its original position to its group-solved final position), and critically the shared corner's INTERMEDIATE (non-final) ring vertex is shared across both faces too, not duplicated per face -- a pre-0458 topology gap this fixture pins down (the pre-fix kernel produced 22v, two coincident-position vertices at the shared corner's t=1 ring).
source: Frozen reference-editor capture via the private capture harness's polygon-bevel op (task 0458 Phase 0 dump-oracle poly_bevel_S1_group_segs2). Orthogonal cube geometry (the group shift accumulator's AVE_N law coincides with the pre-0458 naive sum here -- see poly_bevel_G1_halfshared_tent.json for the asymmetric discriminator), isolating the segments x group interaction specifically.

provenance.harness: private capture harness (reference-editor polygon-bevel tool, headless scripted op-dump)
provenance.notes: Phase-0 dump-oracle for group=true && segments>1, doc'd in mesh.d as KNOWN-UNTESTED before this task. Fixed alongside the group shift-accumulator AVE_N law: the shared corner's intermediate ring vertex is now memoized per ring level (sharedVertIdxByLevel), matching the reference's vertex count (20, not the pre-fix 22).
